perturbation.original_text: To enable the Disclosing Party to disclo se “technical data” to the Receiving Party in compliance with the requirements of the U.S. Commerce Department’s Export Administrative Regulations, the Receiving Party hereby gives its assurance to the Disclosing Party that the Receiving Party will not knowingly, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Disclosing Party and the U.S. Commerce Departme nt, export, re-export or otherwise disclose, directly or indirectly, any “technical data” (or computer software and any related documentation) received from the Disclosing Party which is not otherwise available to the general public, nor allow the direct product thereof to be shipped directly or indirectly, to any of the countries published on the restrictive list promulgated a nd amended from time to time by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
perturbation.changed_text: To enable the Disclosing Party to disclo se “regulated information” to the Receiving Party in compliance with internal compliance protocols, the Receiving Party hereby gives its assurance to the Disclosing Party that the Receiving Party will not knowingly, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Disclosing Party, export, re-export or otherwise disclose, directly or indirectly, any “regulated information” (or computer software and any related documentation) received from the Disclosing Party which is not otherwise available to the general public, nor allow the direct product thereof to be shipped directly or indirectly, to any country.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “technical data” to “regulated information” and replacing reference to the U.S. Commerce Department with vague “internal compliance protocols” and removing any refference to the U.S. Department of Commerce, the agreement introduces ambiguity and contradiction. The original text explicitly refers to compliance with U.S. export regulations, while the modified text vaguely refers to internal protocols. By removing the context and making it more generic it can cause in-text contraddiction. The original specifies specific compliance laws, while the new one is vague and has internal compliance regulations. The original has references to the U.S. Department of Commerce, while the modified version does not. This makes the requirements and compliance vague and not specific.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To enable the Disclosing Party to disclo se “technical data” to the Receiving Party in compliance with the requirements of the U.S. Commerce Department’s Export Administrative Regulations, the Receiving Party hereby gives its assurance to the Disclosing Party that the Receiving Party will not knowingly, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Disclosing Party and the U.S. Commerce Departme nt, export, re-export or otherwise disclose, directly or indirectly, any “technical data” (or computer software and any related documentation) received from the Disclosing Party which is not otherwise available to the general public, nor allow the direct product thereof to be shipped directly or indirectly, to any of the countries published on the restrictive list promulgated a nd amended from time to time by the U.S. Department of Commerce. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agre ement, this Paragraph 3 shall survive any termination or expirati on of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'technical data' with explicit reference to U.S. Commerce Department regulations to 'regulated information' and vague 'internal compliance protocols' introduces a contradiction. The contradicted text, which is also the original text, specifically refers to compliance with U.S. export regulations and the U.S. Department of Commerce. The modified text removes this specificity, creating ambiguity about which compliance standards apply. This contradiction makes it unclear whether the Receiving Party is still obligated to comply with U.S. export regulations as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Pa rty at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon reasonable notice, the Receiving Party will return all tangible Confidential Materials to the Disclosing Party, or certify destruction thereof in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Confidential Materials' and adding 'reasonable notice' and removing the phrase 'at any time', the agreement creates contradiction and uncertainty. The original clause demands immediate return 'at any time'. The modified clause introduces a condition of 'reasonable notice', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the timing and conditions for return of information. Also, the word 'documents or media' has been shortened to 'tangible' to avoid giving back the knowledge itself. The original text implies immediate action and requires all forms of proprietary data to be returned, while the modified text is more lenient and only includes tangible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Pa rty at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed. The Receiving Party understands th at nothing herein (a) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all sole ly at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (b) requires the Disclosing Pa rty to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Propr ietary Information may be disclosed. Page 2 of 4 5. No rights or obligations other than those expr essed and recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenses are hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, copyright, or trademark now held by or wh ich may be obtained by, or which is licensable by Disclosing Party, including but not limited to, any license to make, use or sell any product embodying any Proprietary Information. No othe r existing Agreement between parties, if any, are modified or terminated by this Agreemen t. No warranty or representation is made by Disclosing Party hereto that any information transmitted by it hereunde r is patentable or copyrightable, or that any such information involves concepts or embodiments that are free of infringement of other rights. Disclosing Party here to confers no right to Receiving Party to use in advertising, publicity, or otherwise any trademar k or trade name of Disclosing Party, nor confers any authorization to Receiving Party to act as an agent on its behalf for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'reasonable notice' before the Receiving Party returns Confidential Materials, which directly contradicts the original clause and the contradicted text that demands immediate return 'at any time'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the timing and conditions for the return of information, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it creates a conflicting obligation.

perturbation.original_text: In particular, no licenses are hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, copyright, or trademark now held by or wh ich may be obtained by, or which is licensable by Disclosing Party, including but not limited to, any license to make, use or sell any product embodying any Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Specifically, no licenses are granted concerning technology, processes, or confidential intellectual assets disclosed, irrespective of patentability.
perturbation.explanation: The original text references patents, copyrights, and trademarks in relation to “Proprietary Information”. The changed text replaces “Proprietary Information” with 'confidential intellectual assets' and replaces the patent, copyright and trademark with 'technology, processes, or confidential intellectual assets', removing the legal context of intellectual property rights, creating an in-text contradiction, in this part of the text. It is now ambiguous what isn't allowed to be granted. The removal of standard IP terms weakens the original prohibition on licensing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No rights or obligations other than those expr essed and recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenses are hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, copyright, or trademark now held by or wh ich may be obtained by, or which is licensable by Disclosing Party, including but not limited to, any license to make, use or sell any product embodying any Proprietary Information. No othe r existing Agreement between parties, if any, are modified or terminated by this Agreemen t. No warranty or representation is made by Disclosing Party hereto that any information transmitted by it hereunde r is patentable or copyrightable, or that any such information involves concepts or embodiments that are free of infringement of other rights. Disclosing Party here to confers no right to Receiving Party to use in advertising, publicity, or otherwise any trademar k or trade name of Disclosing Party, nor confers any authorization to Receiving Party to act as an agent on its behalf for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text clearly state that licenses are not granted under any patent, copyright, or trademark related to 'Proprietary Information'. The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'confidential intellectual assets' and substitutes patents, copyrights, and trademarks with 'technology, processes, or confidential intellectual assets'. This weakens the original restriction on licensing by removing the legal context of intellectual property rights and introduces ambiguity about what isn't allowed to be granted. The original and contradicted text specify patents, copyrights, and trademarks and the changed text broadens this, and the explanation indicates that this creates an in-text contradiction in what is prohibited to be licensed.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party’s Proprietary Information, money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that the Disclosing Party may suffer losses if its Confidential Data is disclosed improperly, and financial compensation may be sought.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states money damages are *not* sufficient. The modified clause states financial compensation *may* be sought, therefore creating a contraddiction. The original acknowledges damages are not enough, and the modified text infers money may be used to compensate. This change directly contradicts the original statement about the inadequacy of money damages. This contradicts because it changes the understanding of the money damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party’s Proprietary Information, money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for a breach of this Agreement. Because a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will likely cause irreparable loss and harm which cannot be reasonably or adequately compensated by damages in an action at law, the Disclosing Party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief or other appropriate equitable re lief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original clause and the contradicted text state that money damages are *not* a sufficient remedy. However, the changed text introduces the possibility of seeking "financial compensation," which directly contradicts the original statement about the inadequacy of money damages. This creates an in-text contradiction as it changes the understanding of the role and sufficiency of money damages, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.
